source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Crane/Other Lifting

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Pressurized Gas Release
actions_taken: First aid provided with eye wash bottles and clean bandages
Employee transported to Lafayette General Hospital
Interviews conducted with injured person and helper at Cajun Cutters office
event.summary: On April 4, 2024, at approximately 10:30 PM, an employee of Cajun Cutters Inc. was struck in the left eye and face by a pressurized gas release from the open top of the tree of well A-15 while reconfiguring a tree cap connection. The injured person had been on duty for 15.5 hours. The incident resulted in evacuation, emergency medical treatment, partial permanent hearing loss, and ongoing evaluation for future eye surgeries. Root causes included failure to implement proper Lockout/Tagout procedures, inadequate written procedures, designation of an inexperienced operator for the overnight shift, and lack of safe working surfaces.

threats.name: Failure to isolate well pressure via Lockout/Tagout
Trapped pressure in valve cavity not identified or relieved
Unsafe body positioning due to poor work surfaces

consequences.name: Personnel injury - eye and facial trauma
Long-term disability and ongoing medical treatment

controls.name: Lockout/Tagout of well tree valves
controls.barrier_role: Prevent accidental release of pressure from wellbore and valve cavity
controls.lod_basis: Primary isolation control to prevent pressurized gas release

supporting_text: Lockout/Tagout of the A-15 lower manual master valve was not installed until after the incident occurred
No record of Lockout/Tagout was provided to the BSEE investigators for the automatic surface safety valve
Renaissance did not implement their Lockout/Tagout to ensure positive isolation of all A-15 well tree valves

controls.name: Written procedures for pressure isolation and verification
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all personnel understand which valves are isolated and pressure is removed
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to communicate isolation status and prevent work on pressurized equipment

supporting_text: Renaissance was not able to provide such documents

There were no written procedures provided to the BSEE investigators, nor written instructions provided to personnel that would specify which valves had been isolated and ensure all pressure had been removed

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) with site-specific hazard identification
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures specific to the work task and location
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure hazards are identified and mitigated before work begins

supporting_text: No site specific JSA was prepared discussing job steps or body positioning
The scaffold builder (GSSI), who was assisting with the job, was not listed on the JSA

controls.name: Pressure verification and bleeding procedures
controls.barrier_role: Confirm all pressure has been removed before work on pressurized equipment
controls.lod_basis: Secondary verification control to detect residual pressure before work begins

supporting_text: After verifying the pressure was removed, Cajun Cutters removed a section of pipeline piping
Witnesses stated that the A-15 well had 800 psig of pressure at the top of the well tree before the pressure was bled to zero above the Surface Safety Valve (SSV)
trapped pressure from the valve cavity was released

controls.name: Competent and experienced operator assignment
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operator has platform-specific knowledge and experience
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to ensure qualified personnel manage critical operations

supporting_text: Renaissance designated an operator with no previous platform specific experience as the sole operator on duty of the overnight shift
the operator on duty (Dynamic), during the night shift, had arrived at the platform earlier that day and had never worked at VR-369A previously

controls.name: Safe working surfaces and ergonomic positioning
controls.barrier_role: Provide safe access and positioning to prevent loss of balance and exposure to hazards
controls.lod_basis: Engineering control to reduce exposure to pressurized equipment during work

supporting_text: No safe working surfaces were provided around the entire A-15 well which contributed to the poor body positioning of the IP and helper
The IP was standing with one foot on the SSV the other on the moveable platform and the helper was standing on flowline wing valve of the A-15 well

controls.name: Process alarm detection and response
controls.barrier_role: Detect and alert personnel to unexpected pressure release
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection control to alert personnel to hazardous condition

supporting_text: In more than one statement, personnel claimed to have heard a process alarm moments before the pressure release
Statements included such comments as 'The tree blew up with pressure,' 'It was so loud,' and 'I heard what sounded like a gas release @~10:30 PM'

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) - Eye and face protection
controls.barrier_role: Protect personnel from pressurized gas and debris impact
controls.lod_basis: Tertiary personal protection control to mitigate injury severity

supporting_text: an unexpected release of pressure from the top of the well tree contacted the IP's left eye and the left side of the IP's face

controls.name: Emergency medical response and evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Provide rapid medical treatment and transport to minimize injury severity
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to minimize consequences after incident occurs

supporting_text: The IP was provided first aid with eye wash bottles, clean bandages and Advil
the decision was made to request a medevac flight
The Medevac departed VR-369A with the IP at 1:34 AM and was flown to Lafayette General Hospital to receive emergency treatment

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
